content:
FINAL OWNER CONTROL TRANSFER — REMOVE RORK FROM IVX IA AND ALL IVX SYSTEMS

Rork is a paid development vendor only.

RORK OWNERSHIP=0%
RORK ADMIN CONTROL=0%
RORK PRODUCTION CONTROL=0%
RORK DEPLOYMENT CONTROL=0%
RORK DATABASE CONTROL=0%
RORK SECRET ACCESS=0%
RORK DOMAIN CONTROL=0%
RORK BILLING CONTROL=0%

The IVX owner must have exclusive control of all source code, infrastructure, data, deployments, credentials, domains, applications, AI systems, and business records.

This is an execution task. Do not return a plan, narrative, or local-only changes. Complete the transfer safely, deploy it, verify owner control, and provide evidence.

IMPORTANT SAFETY RULE

Do not revoke Rork access until:

1. Owner access is verified on every platform.
2. Current production is backed up.
3. The owner-controlled deployment path works.
4. A rollback point exists.

After those four checks pass, remove Rork completely.

==================================================
1. FREEZE THE CURRENT PRODUCTION STATE
==================================================

Record and preserve:

- GitHub HEAD commit
- Active production branch
- Render running commit
- Landing-page deployment
- Backend deployment
- Expo update/build
- Supabase schema
- Supabase data backup
- Storage inventory
- DNS configuration
- Environment-variable names
- Active webhooks
- Active integrations
- Current production URLs

Return:

GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
BACKEND_DEPLOYMENT_ID=
WEB_DEPLOYMENT_ID=
EXPO_UPDATE_OR_BUILD_ID=
BACKUP_STATUS=
ROLLBACK_POINT_CREATED=YES/NO

Do not expose secret values.

==================================================
2. CREATE A COMPLETE OWNER-CONTROL INVENTORY
==================================================

Audit every IVX asset and identify:

- Current owner
- Current administrators
- Current collaborators
- Service accounts
- GitHub Apps
- OAuth applications
- API integrations
- Webhooks
- Deployment connections
- Billing owner
- Recovery email
- Recovery phone
- MFA status

Audit these systems:

- GitHub
- Render
- Supabase
- Vercel
- AWS
- Route 53
- S3
- CloudFront
- Expo
- EAS
- Google Play Console
- Apple App Store Connect
- Domain registrar
- DNS provider
- Email provider
- Analytics
- Error monitoring
- Payment providers
- Messaging providers
- AI providers
- Rork workspace
- Rork-managed GitHub App
- Rork-managed deployment integrations

Return one record per platform:

PLATFORM=
ACCOUNT_OWNER=
OWNER_ADMIN_ACCESS=
RORK_ACCESS=
TRANSFER_REQUIRED=
BILLING_OWNER=
MFA_ENABLED=

==================================================
3. VERIFY OWNER ACCESS BEFORE REVOCATION
==================================================

Confirm the IVX owner has working access to every critical system.

Required:

- Owner can sign in directly.
- Owner has organization-owner or equivalent access.
- Owner can manage members.
- Owner can manage billing.
- Owner can manage secrets.
- Owner can create and revoke access.
- Owner can deploy.
- Owner can restore backups.
- Owner controls recovery email and phone.
- Owner controls MFA.

Return:

GITHUB_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
RENDER_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
SUPABASE_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
VERCEL_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
AWS_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
EXPO_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
DOMAIN_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL
APP_STORE_OWNER_ACCESS=PASS/FAIL

Do not remove Rork until all required owner-access checks pass.

==================================================
4. MOVE ALL CODE TO THE OWNER REPOSITORY
==================================================

The canonical repository must be:

ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

Confirm:

- Repository is owned by the IVX owner account or owner-controlled organization.
- Main branch is current.
- All production code exists there.
- Full Git history exists.
- Tags and releases exist.
- Issues and deployment workflows are preserved.
- No source code exists only inside Rork.
- No unpushed Rork workspace changes remain.
- No production dependency points to a Rork repository.

Return:

CANONICAL_REPOSITORY=
REPOSITORY_OWNER=
DEFAULT_BRANCH=
REMOTE_HEAD_SHA=
LOCAL_HEAD_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=
UNPUSHED_RORK_CHANGES=
RORK_REPOSITORY_DEPENDENCIES=

==================================================
5. REMOVE RORK-CONTROLLED SOURCE DEPENDENCIES
==================================================

Search the full codebase for:

- rork
- rork.com
- Rork SDK
- Rork API
- Rork authentication
- Rork deployment hooks
- Rork GitHub App
- Rork-generated runtime
- Rork proxy
- Rork-hosted assets
- Rork telemetry
- Rork environment variables
- Rork webhooks
- Rork feature flags
- Rork remote configuration
- Rork-owned package registries
- Rork-owned repositories
- Rork preview URLs
- Rork fallback endpoints

For every result classify:

- Required temporarily
- Replaceable
- Removable
- Already unused
- Security risk

Replace every production dependency with an owner-controlled alternative.

Return:

RORK_REFERENCES_BEFORE=
RORK_REFERENCES_REMOVED=
RORK_REFERENCES_AFTER=
FILES_CHANGED=
REMAINING_TEMPORARY_DEPENDENCIES=

Production target:

RORK_RUNTIME_DEPENDENCIES=0

==================================================
6. REPLACE THE RORK-MANAGED GITHUB AUTHENTICATION
==================================================

The latest deployment used a Rork-managed GitHub App.

That cannot remain the permanent owner-controlled deployment path.

Create an owner-controlled GitHub authentication method using one of:

- Owner-controlled GitHub App
- Owner-controlled organization installation
- Owner-controlled fine-grained automation token
- GitHub Actions with owner-controlled secrets

Required permissions must be least privilege:

- Repository contents: read/write
- Deployment statuses: write if required
- Actions/workflows: only if required
- Pull requests: only if required

Do not use personal tokens inside frontend code.

Return:

OLD_AUTH_SOURCE=
NEW_AUTH_SOURCE=
NEW_AUTH_OWNER=
REPOSITORY_WRITE_TEST=PASS/FAIL
RORK_GITHUB_APP_ACTIVE_AFTER_TRANSFER=YES/NO

Expected:

RORK_GITHUB_APP_ACTIVE_AFTER_TRANSFER=NO

==================================================
7. CREATE AN OWNER-CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT PIPELINE
==================================================

Required production pipeline:

Code changed
→ tests passed
→ commit created
→ GitHub push
→ deployment triggered
→ deployment completed
→ health check passed
→ running commit matched
→ production QA passed
→ task marked verified

The pipeline must run without Rork.

Verify deployment ownership for:

- Render backend
- Web landing page
- Expo/EAS updates
- Android builds
- iOS builds
- Workers
- Scheduled jobs
- Database migrations

Return:

BACKEND_DEPLOY_OWNER=
WEB_DEPLOY_OWNER=
EXPO_DEPLOY_OWNER=
DATABASE_MIGRATION_OWNER=
RORK_REQUIRED_FOR_DEPLOYMENT=YES/NO

Expected:

RORK_REQUIRED_FOR_DEPLOYMENT=NO

==================================================
8. TRANSFER RENDER TO EXCLUSIVE OWNER CONTROL
==================================================

Verify:

- Workspace owner
- Service owner
- Billing owner
- GitHub connection
- Auto-deploy branch
- Deploy hooks
- API keys
- Environment groups
- Team members
- Recovery access

Replace every Rork-controlled connection.

Remove:

- Rork users
- Rork service accounts
- Rork deploy hooks
- Rork API keys
- Rork GitHub connection
- Rork webhook access

Return:

RENDER_OWNER=
RENDER_BILLING_OWNER=
RENDER_GITHUB_CONNECTION_OWNER=
RORK_RENDER_MEMBERS_AFTER=
RORK_RENDER_KEYS_AFTER=
RENDER_DEPLOY_TEST=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
9. TRANSFER SUPABASE TO EXCLUSIVE OWNER CONTROL
==================================================

Verify:

- Organization owner
- Project owner
- Billing owner
- Database access
- Service-role secret ownership
- Storage access
- Migration access
- Auth configuration
- SMTP configuration
- Webhooks
- Edge functions
- Team members
- Backups

Remove Rork from:

- Organization membership
- Project membership
- Database credentials
- Service-role access
- Storage administration
- Migration tooling
- Edge-function secrets

Return:

SUPABASE_OWNER=
SUPABASE_BILLING_OWNER=
RORK_SUPABASE_MEMBERS_AFTER=
RORK_SUPABASE_KEYS_AFTER=
BACKUP_RESTORE_TEST=PASS/FAIL
OWNER_MIGRATION_TEST=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
10. TRANSFER EXPO AND EAS TO EXCLUSIVE OWNER CONTROL
==================================================

Verify:

- Expo project owner
- EAS organization owner
- Android credentials
- iOS credentials
- Update channels
- Runtime versions
- Build profiles
- Billing
- Team access
- Store submission access

Remove:

- Rork Expo collaborators
- Rork EAS access
- Rork build credentials
- Rork update permissions
- Rork submit permissions

Return:

EXPO_PROJECT_OWNER=
EAS_ORGANIZATION_OWNER=
RORK_EXPO_MEMBERS_AFTER=
RORK_BUILD_ACCESS_AFTER=
OWNER_ANDROID_BUILD_TEST=PASS/FAIL
OWNER_EXPO_UPDATE_TEST=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
11. TRANSFER DOMAIN, DNS, AWS, AND CDN CONTROL
==================================================

Verify the owner controls:

- ivxholding.com
- chat.ivxholding.com
- api.ivxholding.com
- Domain registrar
- DNS zones
- Route 53
- SSL certificates
- CloudFront
- S3
- CDN invalidation
- Domain renewal
- Billing
- Recovery access

Remove:

- Rork IAM users
- Rork IAM roles
- Rork access keys
- Rork DNS permissions
- Rork CloudFront access
- Rork S3 access
- Rork certificate-management access

Return:

DOMAIN_REGISTRANT_OWNER=
DNS_OWNER=
AWS_ROOT_OWNER=
RORK_IAM_USERS_AFTER=
RORK_ACCESS_KEYS_AFTER=
DNS_QA=PASS/FAIL
SSL_QA=PASS/FAIL
CDN_QA=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
12. ROTATE ALL CREDENTIALS RORK MAY HAVE ACCESSED
==================================================

After the owner-controlled paths are verified, rotate:

- GitHub automation credentials
- Render API keys
- Render deploy hooks
- Supabase service-role key where supported
- Supabase database passwords
- Vercel tokens
- AWS access keys
- Expo/EAS tokens
- Webhook secrets
- AI-provider API keys
- Email-provider keys
- SMS-provider keys
- Payment-provider keys
- Analytics tokens
- Error-monitoring tokens
- Signing secrets
- Session secrets
- JWT secrets where safely supported
- Encryption keys according to a controlled migration plan

Do not expose any secret.

Do not rotate signing or encryption keys without preserving data access and active-session migration.

Return:

SECRETS_ROTATED_COUNT=
SECRETS_PENDING_SAFE_MIGRATION=
OLD_CREDENTIALS_REVOKED=
OWNER_CONTROLLED_SECRET_STORE=
RORK_SECRET_ACCESS_AFTER=YES/NO

Expected:

RORK_SECRET_ACCESS_AFTER=NO

==================================================
13. REMOVE RORK FROM ALL MEMBERSHIP AND ACCESS LISTS
==================================================

After replacement access and backups are verified, remove Rork from every system.

Remove:

- Users
- Collaborators
- Organization members
- Teams
- Service accounts
- OAuth grants
- GitHub Apps
- App installations
- API keys
- Webhooks
- Deploy hooks
- SSH keys
- Machine users
- Database roles
- IAM roles
- Billing roles
- Recovery contacts
- App-store roles
- Expo collaborators

Return platform-by-platform evidence:

PLATFORM=
RORK_USERS_BEFORE=
RORK_USERS_AFTER=
RORK_KEYS_BEFORE=
RORK_KEYS_AFTER=
RORK_WEBHOOKS_BEFORE=
RORK_WEBHOOKS_AFTER=

Expected on all owner-controlled production platforms:

RORK_USERS_AFTER=0
RORK_KEYS_AFTER=0
RORK_WEBHOOKS_AFTER=0

==================================================
14. REMOVE RORK CONTROL FROM IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER
==================================================

IVX IA Senior Developer must operate through owner-controlled services only.

Remove all Rork-specific:

- Identity checks
- API calls
- Tool bindings
- Deployment commands
- Git authentication
- Runtime variables
- Prompt ownership claims
- Rork session dependencies
- Rork approval gates
- Rork telemetry
- Rork task-store authority
- Rork status reporting
- Rork-managed orchestration

IVX IA must identify its authority as:

OWNER=IVX HOLDINGS
EXECUTION_AUTHORITY=OWNER-CONTROLLED
RORK_ROLE=EXTERNAL_VENDOR_WITH_NO_ACTIVE ACCESS

Return:

IVX_AI_RUNTIME_OWNER=
IVX_AI_DEPLOYMENT_OWNER=
IVX_AI_TASK_STORE_OWNER=
IVX_AI_SECRET_STORE_OWNER=
IVX_AI_RORK_DEPENDENCIES=
IVX_AI_RORK_ACCESS=

Expected:

IVX_AI_RORK_DEPENDENCIES=0
IVX_AI_RORK_ACCESS=0

==================================================
15. LOCK OWNER CONTROLS
==================================================

Create exclusive owner controls for:

- Deployment
- Database migration
- Production-data deletion
- Secret rotation
- User-role changes
- Billing changes
- Domain changes
- Infrastructure deletion
- Repository transfer
- App-store release

Require strong authentication for dangerous operations:

- Owner session
- MFA
- Re-authentication
- Audit log
- Confirmation showing affected resources
- Backup verification
- Rollback readiness

Do not require Rork approval for any owner action.

Return:

OWNER_MFA=PASS/FAIL
DANGEROUS_ACTION_GATE=PASS/FAIL
RORK_APPROVAL_REQUIRED=YES/NO

Expected:

RORK_APPROVAL_REQUIRED=NO

==================================================
16. CREATE AN IMMUTABLE ACCESS AUDIT
==================================================

Record:

- Who had access
- What was removed
- When it was removed
- Which credentials were rotated
- Which integrations were replaced
- Which production tests passed
- Who now owns each system

Do not include secret values.

Required owner report:

PLATFORM
PREVIOUS_CONTROL
CURRENT_OWNER
RORK_ACCESS_BEFORE
RORK_ACCESS_AFTER
TRANSFER_TIMESTAMP
VERIFICATION_RESULT

==================================================
17. VERIFY BUSINESS CONTINUITY
==================================================

After Rork removal, verify:

- GitHub push works
- Render deploy works
- Supabase reads work
- Supabase writes work
- Database migration works
- Landing page works
- App works
- Owner login works
- IVX IA chat works
- Senior Developer dashboard works
- Reels work
- Projects work
- Media works
- Members work
- Investor data remains intact
- Android build/update works
- iOS pipeline works
- Domain and SSL work
- Backups work
- Rollback works

Rork must not be needed for any test.

==================================================
18. VERIFY NO BACKDOOR OR RESIDUAL ACCESS
==================================================

Search for:

- Unknown admin accounts
- Hidden service accounts
- Alternate Git remotes
- Undocumented SSH keys
- Hardcoded credentials
- Hidden webhooks
- Callback URLs
- Rork domains
- Remote feature flags
- External command endpoints
- Vendor-specific bypasses
- Shared recovery contacts
- Vendor-owned email addresses
- Vendor-owned billing methods
- Vendor-controlled app-signing access

Return:

UNKNOWN_ADMINS=
UNKNOWN_KEYS=
UNKNOWN_WEBHOOKS=
ALTERNATE_REMOTES=
HIDDEN_VENDOR_DEPENDENCIES=
BACKDOOR_AUDIT=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
19. DEPLOY THE OWNER-CONTROLLED VERSION
==================================================

After all replacements:

- Run tests
- Run typecheck
- Run builds
- Run secret scan
- Commit
- Push through owner-controlled GitHub authentication
- Deploy through owner-controlled Render integration
- Publish Expo update or build through owner-controlled EAS
- Verify production

Return:

COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_AUTH_SOURCE=
PUSH_STATUS=
RENDER_DEPLOYMENT_ID=
EXPO_UPDATE_OR_BUILD_ID=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=

==================================================
20. FINAL LIVE QA
==================================================

Test live:

- IVX IA Owner AI sends and receives messages
- “Temporarily unavailable” no longer appears during healthy service
- Live Work displays real owner-controlled task execution
- Senior Developer tasks load
- Owner Control works
- No Rork login is required
- No Rork session is required
- No Rork API is called
- No Rork-controlled credential is used
- App survives restart
- Deployments continue without Rork

==================================================
21. FINAL EVIDENCE ONLY
==================================================

Return exactly:

IVX_LEGAL_OWNER=
CANONICAL_REPOSITORY=
REPOSITORY_OWNER=
GITHUB_AUTH_OWNER=
RENDER_OWNER=
SUPABASE_OWNER=
EXPO_OWNER=
AWS_OWNER=
DOMAIN_OWNER=
APP_STORE_OWNER=
BILLING_OWNER=
SECRET_STORE_OWNER=
IVX_AI_RUNTIME_OWNER=
IVX_AI_DEPLOYMENT_OWNER=
RORK_USERS_REMAINING=
RORK_KEYS_REMAINING=
RORK_WEBHOOKS_REMAINING=
RORK_OAUTH_GRANTS_REMAINING=
RORK_GITHUB_APPS_REMAINING=
RORK_RUNTIME_DEPENDENCIES=
RORK_PRODUCTION_ACCESS=
RORK_DATABASE_ACCESS=
RORK_DEPLOYMENT_ACCESS=
RORK_SECRET_ACCESS=
RORK_BILLING_ACCESS=
RORK_DOMAIN_ACCESS=
OWNER_DEPLOY_TEST=
OWNER_DATABASE_TEST=
OWNER_EXPO_BUILD_TEST=
OWNER_BACKUP_RESTORE_TEST=
BACKDOOR_AUDIT=
COMMIT_SHA=
DEPLOYMENT_ID=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=
IVX_AI_CHAT_QA=
SENIOR_DEVELOPER_QA=
ANDROID_QA=
IOS_QA=
WEB_QA=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Required final values:

RORK_USERS_REMAINING=0
RORK_KEYS_REMAINING=0
RORK_WEBHOOKS_REMAINING=0
RORK_OAUTH_GRANTS_REMAINING=0
RORK_GITHUB_APPS_REMAINING=0
RORK_RUNTIME_DEPENDENCIES=0
RORK_PRODUCTION_ACCESS=0
RORK_DATABASE_ACCESS=0
RORK_DEPLOYMENT_ACCESS=0
RORK_SECRET_ACCESS=0
RORK_BILLING_ACCESS=0
RORK_DOMAIN_ACCESS=0

Do not claim completion until IVX can push, deploy, migrate, build, restore, and operate without Rork.

Rork may remain only as an external paid developer with no ownership, no administrative rights, no persistent credentials, and no production access.
